meaning: n.唾液v.吐(唾沫),吐出,发射,发出,喷吐
examples: Notices in the waiting room requested that you neither smoke nor spit.
He spit it out, saying it was awful.
He'll make a fine fire for this spit.
When they brushed their teeth, most of the time they spit in the toilet.
'If you fall in, you'll probably get spit out eventually, ' he says with a smile.
